agent.runner_summary: Runs the OpenAI Agents SDK airline support agent through the RELAI simulator adapter with raw user-string turns.

components.when_to_use: Use for booking lookup correctness, confirmation-code validation, or booking-specific response issues.
components.input_guidance: Use named kwargs that match the tool schema, for example {"confirmation_code": "SKY123"}.
components.output_guidance: Evaluate the returned booking summary or missing-booking message.

components.when_to_use: Use for baggage-policy behavior or ticket-type interpretation issues.
components.input_guidance: Use named kwargs matching the tool schema, typically {"ticket_type": "basic economy"}.
components.output_guidance: Evaluate the returned baggage-policy text for the requested fare type.

components.when_to_use: Use for seat-change flows, confirmation-code handling, or validation of requested seat updates.
components.input_guidance: Use named kwargs matching the tool schema, for example {"confirmation_code": "SKY123", "requested_seat": "14C"}.
components.output_guidance: Evaluate the seat-update confirmation or the invalid-booking error message.

log_notes: Agent learning environments should pass each user turn as a raw string. The adapter keeps prior user and assistant turns as conversation history.
FunctionTool component targets use named JSON-schema kwargs, not positional args.
Mocked tool invocations appear under the target strings listed in .relai/mock-manifest.json.
learning_env_guidance: Use a plain string for FixedTurn.content, such as a normal customer question. Do not wrap the turn in a message object.
Multi-turn agent environments can supply multiple user-string turns; the adapter preserves conversation history between turns.
Set OPENAI_API_KEY or ANTHROPIC_API_KEY before running the simulator. When both are set, the project selects OpenAI; otherwise it uses the Anthropic LiteLLM model defined by the project.
